seoTitle: How to Update n8n Locally Using the Terminal
metaDescription: Learn how to safely stop, update, and restart a local npm-installed n8n instance from your terminal to get the latest version.
intro: If you're running n8n locally through npm, keeping it updated means working from your terminal rather than clicking through a settings menu. This guide covers stopping the running server, pulling the latest package, and verifying your instance upgraded successfully.

steps.heading: Check your current version
steps.body: Look at your running n8n dashboard to note the version you're currently on before updating.

steps.heading: Stop the running server
steps.body: Open your terminal and stop the active local n8n process to free up the port.

steps.heading: Run the update command
steps.body: Use the appropriate npm update command to pull down the latest n8n package.

steps.heading: Restart the server
steps.body: Launch n8n again from the terminal so it starts on the newly installed version.

steps.heading: Verify the upgrade
steps.body: Open your local dashboard in the browser and confirm the version number has changed.

faq.question: Do I need to stop n8n before updating it locally?
faq.answer: Yes, stopping the running process first helps avoid port conflicts during the update.

faq.question: What command updates a local npm installation of n8n?
faq.answer: You'll run the standard npm global update command targeting the n8n package from your terminal.

faq.question: Will updating n8n affect my existing workflows?
faq.answer: Your workflow data is generally preserved, but it's wise to check for any migration notes tied to the new version.

faq.question: How do I confirm the update worked?
faq.answer: Open your local dashboard after restarting and check that the displayed version number has updated.
